title: thymus serpyllum subsp. marginatus
intro: Thymus serpyllum subsp. marginatus, also known as creeping thyme or wild thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant, characterized by its low-growing habit and spreading habit. This herb has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds that help combat infections, reduce pain, and soothe digestive issues. The health benefits of thymus serpyllum subsp. marginatus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve respiratory health, making it a popular natural remedy for colds and coughs. Thymus serpyllum subsp. marginatus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to address a range of health concerns.
